fields.description:
## Context / Problem
At current origin/main commit 0f5dbc88b, Ruff ASYNC emits zero diagnostics across `src/rebar`. Ruff DTZ emits exactly three diagnostics: DTZ006 for `datetime.fromtimestamp()` and DTZ007 for `datetime.strptime()` in `src/rebar/_engine_support/validate_checks.py`, plus DTZ011 for `date.today()` in `src/rebar/grounding/detectors/security_pin.py`. The approved ratchet should land only after all three sites have explicit, tested timezone semantics.

## Dependencies and Ownership
This story has an ordering-only `depends_on` edge to `d9f5-baa5-9fc3-451d` because both stories edit `pyproject.toml`; the Mypy strictness configuration lands first to avoid sibling merge conflicts. No runtime behavior in this story depends on the Mypy change. This story creates and owns `tests/unit/test_ruff_ratchet.py`; `125d-ee6f-7af0-4200` depends on this story and extends that same file later.

## Approach
1. Create `tests/unit/test_ruff_ratchet.py` as the owner of the shared Ruff configuration contract. This test separately asserts ASYNC selection, DTZ selection, absence of the exact Ruff security selector `S`, absence of `.semgrep.yml`, `.semgrep.yaml`, and `.semgrep/`, and absence of the literal `datetime.UTC` token from production Python files under `src/rebar`.
2. Define validation timestamp semantics as UTC. Change `validate_checks.py` to use the datetime module. Integer epoch seconds are converted with `datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(created, tz=datetime.timezone.utc)`. Persisted timestamp strings without an offset are interpreted as the event store's UTC wall time with `datetime.datetime.strptime(...).replace(tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)`. Both paths produce the same UTC hourly cluster key.
3. Prove timezone invariance on supported POSIX/macOS platforms by running the validation behavior under at least two `TZ` values with `time.tzset()`. The test is explicitly skipped when `time.tzset` is unavailable; Windows is not a declared supported platform in `pyproject.toml`. Rejected: adding a production timezone-injection seam solely for this test, because the explicit timezone argument is directly testable and an extra API would add unused production complexity.
4. Preserve security-rule cadence as a host-local calendar-date decision. Obtain an aware current instant with an explicit `tz` argument, convert it to the host timezone, then extract its date. The implementation may use an equivalent helper or expression; the contract is the host-local date semantics plus DTZ005 compliance, not a particular line spelling. Boundary tests cover age equal to `cadence_days` and age greater than `cadence_days`.
5. Add ASYNC and DTZ to the gated Ruff selection after the tests fail against current configuration.
6. Run the focused, default, and integration pytest tiers.

The spelling `datetime.UTC` is prohibited because Ruff `target-version` remains `py310`; all production UTC construction uses `datetime.timezone.utc`. Rejected: blanket DTZ ignores.

## Scope
- `pyproject.toml`
- `tests/unit/test_ruff_ratchet.py` (required; created and owned by this story)
- `src/rebar/_engine_support/validate_checks.py`
- `src/rebar/grounding/detectors/security_pin.py`
- `tests/unit/test_validate_idea_exemptions.py`
- `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws5.py`

## Testing / Verification
- Configuration and negative assertions: `pytest -q tests/unit/test_ruff_ratchet.py`
- Focused behavior: `pytest -q tests/unit/test_validate_idea_exemptions.py tests/unit/test_code_review_ws5.py`
- Ruff families: `ruff check --select ASYNC,DTZ src/rebar tests`
- Canonical lint: `make lint`
- Default tier: `pytest -m "not integration and not external" -q`
- Integration tier: `pytest -m integration -q`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_ruff_ratchet.py` asserts ASYNC is selected by the global Ruff configuration.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_ruff_ratchet.py` asserts DTZ is selected by the global Ruff configuration.
- [ ] `ruff check --select ASYNC,DTZ src/rebar tests` exits 0.
- [ ] Tests prove integer epoch timestamps produce the same UTC hour cluster under at least two distinct process `TZ` settings on platforms providing `time.tzset`.
- [ ] Tests prove timezone-less persisted timestamp strings are assigned `datetime.timezone.utc` before clustering.
- [ ] A source-level AST assertion verifies the UTC-producing calls in `validate_checks.py` use `datetime.timezone.utc`; `pytest -q tests/unit/test_validate_idea_exemptions.py` executes that assertion.
- [ ] Behavioral validation tests prove the numeric and timezone-less timestamp paths produce the expected UTC cluster keys; `pytest -q tests/unit/test_validate_idea_exemptions.py` exits 0.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_ruff_ratchet.py` recursively scans `src/rebar` Python files and fails if the literal `datetime.UTC` token is present.
- [ ] Security-pin tests prove the implementation obtains an aware current instant with an explicit timezone, converts it to the host timezone, and uses the resulting local date; `pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_ws5.py` exits 0.
- [ ] `ruff check --select DTZ005 src/rebar/grounding/detectors/security_pin.py` exits 0.
- [ ] Security-pin tests prove `age_days == cadence_days` is not stale.
- [ ] Security-pin tests prove `age_days > cadence_days` is stale.
- [ ] `make lint` exits 0.
- [ ] `pytest -m "not integration and not external" -q` exits 0.
- [ ] `pytest -m integration -q` exits 0.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_ruff_ratchet.py` asserts the exact Ruff security selector `S` is absent.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_ruff_ratchet.py` asserts `.semgrep.yml`, `.semgrep.yaml`, and `.semgrep/` are absent.
